definition: An entity that bears responsibility for activities and can act on behalf of itself or others.
notes: Umbrella class; subclasses are BFO-aligned. Aligns with prov:Agent.

synonyms: software agent, AI assistant, LLM agent
definition: An agent that is a software system capable of autonomous decision-making and tool use.
notes: Aligns with prov:SoftwareAgent. Information content entity.

definition: An agent that is a human person who interacts with AI systems.

synonyms: child agent, delegated agent, spawned agent
definition: An AI agent that was spawned by another agent to perform delegated work.
notes: Inherits GDC from AIAgent. Has spawnedBy link to parent.

definition: A role that an agent plays in the context of a conversation or activity.
notes: Aligns with prov:Role. Closed set of individuals via owl:oneOf.

synonyms: tool schema, tool descriptor, tool specification
definition: A generically dependent continuant that specifies the interface, capabilities, and constraints of an available tool.
notes: Describes tool name and JSON schema; does not model implementation.

synonyms: tool call, tool use, tool execution
definition: A process in which an agent requests execution of a tool by providing inputs and receiving outputs.
notes: Subclass of Action. N-ary pattern with input/output/status.

definition: A process consisting of a coherent thread of dialogue between participants that may span multiple sessions.
A process that is a bounded period of agent-user interaction within a conversation.
notes: Temporal part of a Conversation. Has status (Active/Ended/Interrupted).

definition: A process that is a single contribution by one participant in a conversation.
notes: Temporal part of a Session. Has turn index for ordering.

definition: A generically dependent continuant that is the textual or structured content of a conversational turn.
notes: Information content distinct from the Turn (process) that produced it.

definition: A process in which an agent's context window is compressed by summarizing prior turns.
notes: Subclass of Event. Produces a summary (MemoryItem).

definition: A generically dependent continuant that is an information artifact persisted in an agent's memory system.
notes: Aligns with prov:Entity. Uses ODP-4 (Information Realization).

synonyms: memory layer, memory level, storage tier
definition: A generically dependent continuant that defines a layer in the agent's memory architecture with specific storage and access characteristics.

synonyms: context window, short-term memory, in-context memory
definition: A memory tier that holds information currently active in the agent's context window.
notes: Maps to LLM context window. Most volatile tier.

synonyms: experience memory, event log, recall memory
definition: A memory tier that stores time-situated experiences and interaction histories.

synonyms: fact memory, knowledge base, long-term knowledge
definition: A memory tier that stores generalized facts, knowledge, and beliefs extracted from experience.
notes: Contains Claims. Letta: archival storage for facts.

definition: A memory tier that stores learned action policies, skills, and behavioral patterns.
notes: System prompts and tool-use policies. Least volatile tier.

definition: A memory item that records a temporally bounded cluster of related events.
notes: Subclass of MemoryItem only (not Event). See bfo-alignment.md for rationale.

definition: A memory item that represents an agent-held proposition with associated confidence, evidence, and provenance metadata.
A status class whose individuals classify the type of a claim.
A process that acts on memory items to encode, retrieve, consolidate, or remove them.
A memory operation that writes new information into a memory tier.
A memory operation that reads information from a memory tier.
A memory operation that transforms episodic memories into semantic memories.
A memory operation that removes or decays memory items from a memory tier.
notes: Includes passive decay and active deletion.

definition: A process that is an occurrence situated in time within the agent system.
An event that is intentionally caused by an agent to affect the environment or internal state.
notes: Subclass of Event. Has performedBy link to Agent.

definition: A generically dependent continuant that specifies a desired state an agent works toward.
notes: BDI goal concept. Has status and pursuedBy agent.

definition: A generically dependent continuant that specifies an ordered sequence of intended actions to achieve a goal.
notes: Aligns with prov:Plan. Has tasks as parts.

definition: A generically dependent continuant that is a concrete actionable work item within a plan with status and dependencies.
notes: Component of a Plan. Has blockedBy dependencies.

definition: A generically dependent continuant that specifies rules governing what actions an agent is permitted or forbidden to perform.
A generically dependent continuant that specifies a non-negotiable behavioral limit on an agent.
notes: Stronger than PermissionPolicy; cannot be overridden.

definition: A process in which memory items are identified and deleted to comply with privacy requirements.
notes: Subclass of Event. Records who requested and when.

definition: A class whose individuals represent the current state of an entity.
A status applicable to sessions indicating whether the session is active, ended, or interrupted.
A status applicable to tasks indicating progress from pending through completion.
A status indicating whether a tool invocation complied with applicable permission policies.
Status value: an entity is currently active.
Status value: a session has ended normally.
Status value: a session ended abnormally.
Status value: a task has not yet started.
Status value: a task is currently being worked on.
Status value: a task or process has finished successfully.
Status value: a task cannot proceed because of unresolved dependencies.
Compliance status: a tool invocation followed all applicable policies.
Compliance status: a tool invocation violated one or more policies.
The role of the AI assistant in a conversation.
The role of the human user in a conversation.
A claim type indicating the claim records a user preference.
notes: Used as a value for claimType property.

definition: DEPRECATED (v0.2.0): replaced by SensitivityLevel individuals (Public/Internal/Confidential/Restricted). Was a sensitivity classifier for hasSensitivityLevel.
notes: Replaced by SensitivityLevel value partition in v0.2.0. Maps approximately to Confidential.

definition: An agent that is a formal body or group of people.
notes: Aligns with prov:Organization. Subclass of Agent.

synonyms: system prompt, agent identity, configured persona
definition: A GDC that specifies an agent's configured identity behavior and system prompt.
An event in which an agent receives environmental feedback.
notes: Subclass of Event. Linked to Session via inSession.

definition: A memory operation that strengthens a memory item via repeated access.
notes: Subclass of MemoryOperation. Added to DisjointUnion.

synonyms: core memory block, key-value memory, Letta block
definition: A memory item that stores structured key-value data (Letta core memory pattern).
notes: Subclass of MemoryItem. Has hasBlockKey and hasBlockValue.

definition: A GDC representing an agent's commitment to execute a plan (BDI model).
notes: Linked to Goal via derivedFromGoal and to Agent via intendedBy.

definition: A status class whose individuals classify privacy sensitivity.
A GDC that records a data subject's consent for a specific purpose.
A GDC specifying how long memory items should be retained before deletion.
Sensitivity level: unrestricted data.
Sensitivity level: organization-internal data.
Sensitivity level: restricted personal data.
Sensitivity level: highly sensitive data requiring special controls.
A process in which an entity's status changes from one value to another.
notes: Subclass of Event. Reified transition with fromStatus/toStatus.

definition: A status transition in which a session's status changes.
A status transition in which a task's status changes.
An information artifact recording the fate of an individual item during a compaction event.
notes: N-ary reification linking event to item and fate.

definition: A status class whose individuals classify what happened to an item during compaction.
Item fate: content incorporated into summary.
Item fate: excluded from summary.
Item fate: partially represented in summary.
Item fate: moved to long-term storage.

synonyms: context buffer, attention window, token window
definition: A GDC representing the finite attention resource of an AI agent session with token capacity and usage tracking.
notes: Linked to Session via hasContextWindow. Linked to CompactionEvent via compactedContextOf.

definition: A GDC that specifies a configured communication endpoint through which agent-user dialogue occurs.
notes: Linked to Session via viaChannel. Has a ChannelType classification.

definition: A status class whose individuals classify the type of communication channel.
A GDC that represents a configured connection to an external service that provides one or more tools.
notes: Linked to Agent via hasIntegration. Provides tools via providesTool.

definition: A status class whose individuals classify the operational state of an integration.
Channel type: command-line interface interaction.

synonyms: chat app, instant messaging, WhatsApp, Slack, Discord
definition: Channel type: messaging platform interaction.
Channel type: web-based chat interface.
Channel type: programmatic API access.
notes: Named individual of ChannelType. Named APIChannel to avoid collision with generic API term.

definition: Channel type: voice-based interaction.
notes: Named individual of ChannelType. Named VoiceChannel for clarity.

definition: Channel type: email-based interaction.
notes: Named individual of ChannelType. Named EmailChannel for clarity.

definition: The display name of the external service an integration connects to.
notes: Functional data property on Integration. Required (minCount 1 in SHACL).

definition: The endpoint URI of the external service an integration connects to.
notes: Functional data property on Integration. Optional (some local integrations have no network endpoint). Range: xsd:anyURI.

definition: Integration status: the integration is connected and operational.
Integration status: the integration is not currently connected.
Integration status: the integration has encountered an error.
Integration status: the integration is being set up.
A GDC that describes a configured external service with identity and transport metadata.
notes: Module: pao-services. Richer than Integration — models capability topology.

definition: A GDC representing the runtime connection between an agent and an external service.
A GDC representing a capability that an external service exposes (abstract parent).
notes: Module: pao-services. DisjointUnion of tool/resource/prompt subtypes.

definition: A service capability that provides a tool for agent invocation.
A service capability that provides a data resource for agent access.
A service capability that provides a prompt template.
An event in which an agent discovers what capabilities an external service exposes.
A status class whose individuals classify runtime connection state.
notes: Module: pao-services. Individuals: Open; Closed; Reconnecting; Failed.

definition: Links an agent to an external service it is configured to use.
Links an agent to a runtime service connection.
Links a service connection to the external service it connects to.
Links an external service to a capability it exposes.
Links a discovery event to a capability that was discovered.
Links a discovery event to the service it discovered capabilities from.
Links a service connection to its runtime status.
notes: Domain: ServiceConnection. Range: ConnectionStatus. Functional. SubPropertyOf: hasStatus.

definition: The transport protocol of an external service (e.g. stdio; sse; http).
A unique identifier string for an external service.
Connection status: the connection is open and active.
Connection status: the connection is closed.
Connection status: the connection is attempting to reconnect.
Connection status: the connection has failed.
A GDC specifying the permission enforcement level under which an agent operates.
notes: Module: pao-governance. Value partition with individuals.

definition: A GDC specifying filesystem and network restrictions imposed on an agent.
A GDC representing a configured interceptor that can modify or block tool invocations.
An event in which a hook intercepts and processes a tool invocation.
A GDC that serves as a container for audit entries.
A GDC recording an authorization decision for a tool invocation with rationale.
A status class whose individuals classify authorization outcomes.
notes: Module: pao-governance. Individuals: Allow; Deny; RequireApproval.

definition: Links an AI agent to its active permission mode.
Links an AI agent to the sandbox policy that constrains it.
Links an agent to a configured hook.
Links a hook to an execution it triggered.
Links a hook execution to the tool invocation it intercepted.
Links an audit entry to the authorization decision recorded.
Links an audit entry to the tool invocation being audited.
Links an audit entry to the audit log it belongs to.
The textual rationale for an authorization decision.
Permission mode: tools are approved automatically.
Permission mode: tools require explicit approval.
Permission mode: tools are denied unless explicitly allowed.
Authorization decision: the operation was permitted.
Authorization decision: the operation was denied.
Authorization decision: the operation requires human approval.
An event in which an agent initiates recovery after a failure.
An event representing a retry of a failed operation.
An event in which an agent generates a new plan after a failure.
An event in which an agent reverts changes made by a failed operation.
A GDC marking a task decision point where human approval may be required.
A status class whose individuals classify checkpoint decision outcomes.
notes: Module: pao-recovery. Individuals: Approved; Rejected; Deferred.

definition: Links a recovery event to the entity (invocation; integration; session) it is recovering from.
Links a recovery event to a retry attempt it spawned.
Links a recovery event to a replan event it triggered.
Links a recovery event to a rollback event it triggered.
Links a checkpoint to the task it gates.
Links a checkpoint to its decision outcome.
The number of retry attempts made.
A textual description of the recovery outcome.
Checkpoint decision: the task was approved to continue.
Checkpoint decision: the task was rejected.
Checkpoint decision: the decision was deferred for later.
A prov:Entity that represents the typed output artifact produced by a tool invocation.
notes: Information content entity carrying invocation output.

definition: A prov:Entity that groups tool invocations executed in parallel within a single turn.
A prov:Entity that represents a typed segment of a message's content.
A status class whose individuals classify the type of a content block.
A status class whose individuals classify where a memory item originates.
A status class whose individuals classify the visibility scope of a memory item.
A prov:Entity that represents a memory artifact accessible to multiple agents for shared read/write.
notes: Standalone GDC; links to agents via sharedAcrossAgents.

definition: An event in which multiple agents attempt concurrent writes to the same shared memory artifact.
A prov:Entity that represents the pragmatic classification of a turn's communicative intent.
notes: Inspired by DIT++ and ISO 24617-2 dialog act taxonomy.

definition: A status class whose individuals classify the communicative purpose of a dialog act.
notes: Curated core subset: Inform; Request; Confirm; Clarify; Accept; Reject.

definition: A prov:Entity that represents the accumulated shared knowledge state between conversation participants.
notes: Inspired by Clark & Schaefer (1989) grounding theory.

definition: An event in which a participant contributes evidence to the common ground of a conversation.
A prov:Entity that records a request for additional information to resolve ambiguity.
notes: Links to the turn being clarified.

definition: A prov:Entity that records evidence that a participant has accepted or understood a proposition.
Links a tool invocation to the group it was executed within.
Links a tool invocation group to the turn in which it was executed.
Links a tool invocation to the result artifact it produced.
Links a message to one of its typed content blocks.
Links a content block to its type classification.
Links a memory item to its originating source classification.
Links a memory item to its visibility scope classification.
Links a shared memory artifact to an agent that has access to it.
Links a memory write conflict to the agent that caused the conflicting write.
Links a memory write conflict to the shared artifact where the conflict occurred.
Links a memory write conflict to the policy used to resolve it.
Links a turn to its pragmatic dialog act classification.
Links a dialog act to its communicative function classification.
Links a grounding act to the common ground it updates.
Links a grounding act to the acceptance evidence it produces.
Links a clarification request to the turn it is clarifying.
The zero-based position of a content block within its parent message.
Content block type: plain text content.
Content block type: a tool invocation request.
Content block type: tool invocation result output.
Content block type: an image or visual content.
Memory source: originated from user input.
Memory source: originated from system processes.
Memory source: originated from agent reasoning.
Memory scope: visible only within a single session.
Memory scope: visible across sessions within a project.
Memory scope: visible across all projects and sessions.
Communicative function: conveying information.
Communicative function: requesting action or information.
Communicative function: confirming understanding or agreement.
Communicative function: seeking to resolve ambiguity.
Communicative function: accepting a proposition into common ground.
Communicative function: rejecting a proposition.
